name: Montana Capital Car Title Loans
description_short: Montana Capital offers car title loans from $100–$50,000 with same-day funding. No credit check required; vehicle equity and income determine approval.
description_long:
Montana Capital Car Title Loans has operated as a title lending agency since 2007, reporting that it has served more than 375,000 borrowers over that period. The company is headquartered in California and operates physical branch locations in addition to an online application channel, giving borrowers multiple ways to access funds. It holds a 4.5-star Google rating based on 59 published reviews.

The company offers secured title loans ranging from $100 to $50,000, using the borrower's vehicle title as collateral. Approval is based on vehicle equity and the applicant's income rather than credit score, which means people with bad credit histories or prior bankruptcies are explicitly welcomed. The application process is advertised as free, with pre-approval in under five minutes and no hard credit inquiry. Funds can be received the same day, either as a direct deposit or collected at a branch location. Borrowers retain possession of their vehicle throughout the repayment term and receive their title back upon final payment.

Montana Capital distinguishes itself primarily through its loan ceiling ($50,000 is substantially higher than most storefront title lenders), its accessible hours noted positively by reviewers, and the speed of its process. Multiple customer reviews cite same-hour or same-day disbursement. One reviewer notes that funds are issued as a Bank of America check rather than cash, which may matter depending on the borrower's circumstances. Staff responsiveness and clear communication are recurring themes in the review set.

Title loans are among the most expensive forms of consumer credit available. Montana Capital does not disclose APRs, interest rates, or fee schedules anywhere on its public website, which is a significant transparency gap. Loan terms secured against a vehicle create repossession risk if the borrower defaults. The loan structure is appropriate only as a last resort for someone who owns a paid-off or nearly paid-off vehicle, cannot qualify for lower-cost alternatives, and is confident in their ability to repay on schedule. The 375,000 customers-served figure is not independently verifiable.
address: 2514 W Berry St #1, Fort Worth, TX 76109

company_info.headquarters: 2514 W Berry St #1, Fort Worth, TX 76109

pros: Loan ceiling of $50,000 — significantly higher than typical storefront title lenders
Same-day funding available; multiple reviewers report cash within one hour of approval
No credit check and no hard inquiry; bad credit and bankruptcies explicitly accepted
Free pre-approval in under 5 minutes with no credit score impact
Borrower keeps the vehicle and continues driving during the repayment period
Operating since 2007 with 375,000+ reported customers — established track record
Online applications available 24/7 with in-person branch pickup option
cons: No APR, interest rate, or fee information disclosed anywhere on the website
Vehicle repossession risk if the borrower defaults — collateral is the car title
Funds disbursed as a Bank of America check, not cash — inconvenient for unbanked borrowers
Only 59 Google reviews despite claiming 375,000 customers served — thin verifiable social proof
Title loans are among the highest-cost loan products; typically carry triple-digit APRs
best_for: Vehicle owners with poor credit or past bankruptcy who cannot qualify for personal loans
Borrowers needing more than $1,000 quickly who are ineligible for payday alternatives
People who own their car outright or have significant equity and need same-day cash
Borrowers comfortable with secured lending who are confident in their repayment ability
diagnosis: Montana Capital is best suited to vehicle owners with poor or no credit who need fast access to cash above what payday lenders offer and cannot qualify for personal loans. The main caveat is that the company does not disclose its rates or APRs publicly, and title loans as a product class carry high costs and vehicle repossession risk — borrowers should exhaust lower-cost options first.
services: Title loans for borrowers with prior bankruptcies
Flexible repayment plans based on borrower income
